events.note: One entry, two national cries — so it appears on each country's day rather than being forced to choose one.

retiredReason: Formula. Out of scope for v1 — RECOVER IMMEDIATELY if the tear-off calendar goes ahead; a dated page needs dated content.

review.notes: Authored as a schema demo; sources not human-verified; reviewer sign-off pending.

es.meaning: The cry of the Grito, on the night of 15 September.
es.exampleTranslation: The president cried: Long live Mexico!
es.regionNote: Mexico-specific. The Grito is the night of the 15th; the holiday is the 16th.

it.exampleTranslation: Long live Italy, long live the Republic!

bridge.note: Identical construction: a fossilised subjunctive of 'to live' used as a cheer. Spanish keeps the accent (¡Viva!), Italian does not. Note the two nations do not share a date — this entry attaches to BOTH occasions and surfaces twice a year.

interference.it_to_es: Italians forget the opening ¡ in Spanish.
